title: Advanced Relationship Topics Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on complex database relationship patterns like self-referencing and polymorphism.

questions.question: What is a 'Self-Referencing' relationship?
options: A relationship where a table relates to itself
A relationship that doesn't need primary keys
A relationship within a single column

questions.question: Which of these is a classic example of a self-referencing relationship?
Which relationship type allows a single model to belong to more than one other model on a single association?
Regarding junction (join) tables, when would you need to add extra attributes beyond just the primary keys?
options: When the relationship itself has properties, such as a 'grade' for a student's enrollment
Never; join tables should only have IDs
